metadata.description: Manually curated ground-truth dataset for end-to-end ClaimSense pipeline evaluation
metadata.sources: UN data, WHO, government reports, verified news, academic records

claims.text: India is the most populous country in the world.
claims.rationale: India surpassed China as most populous country in April 2023 per UN data.

claims.text: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
claims.rationale: Debunked myth. NASA and astronauts including Chinese taikonaut Yang Liwei confirmed it is not visible.

claims.text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
claims.rationale: Standard physics fact.

claims.text: The COVID-19 vaccine contains microchips to track people.
claims.rationale: Repeatedly debunked by WHO, CDC, and independent fact-checkers worldwide.

claims.text: India launched the Chandrayaan-3 mission to the Moon in 2023.
claims.rationale: Chandrayaan-3 launched July 14 2023 and landed near south pole on Aug 23 2023.

claims.text: The Amazon river is the longest river in the world.
claims.rationale: The Nile is generally considered longer. Some studies suggest Amazon may be longer but scientific consensus still favours Nile.

claims.text: Albert Einstein failed mathematics in school.
claims.rationale: Widely circulated myth. Einstein excelled at mathematics from a young age.

claims.text: India became a member of the United Nations in 1945.
claims.rationale: India was a founding member of the UN in 1945 despite being under British rule.

claims.text: 5G technology causes cancer.
claims.rationale: WHO and ICNIRP have found no evidence that 5G radio waves cause cancer at permitted exposure levels.

claims.text: The human body has 206 bones.
claims.rationale: Standard anatomy fact for adult human skeleton.

claims.text: India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2023.
claims.rationale: Australia won the 2023 ICC Cricket World Cup, defeating India in the final.

claims.text: The Paris Agreement was signed in 2015.
claims.rationale: The Paris Agreement was adopted on December 12 2015 at COP21.

claims.text: Elon Musk acquired Twitter and rebranded it to X.
claims.rationale: Musk completed acquisition in October 2022 and rebranded platform to X in 2023.

claims.text: Drinking eight glasses of water per day is a scientific recommendation.
claims.rationale: The 8-glasses rule has no strong scientific basis. Water needs vary by individual, climate, and activity.

claims.text: India's GST was implemented on July 1, 2017.
claims.rationale: Goods and Services Tax was officially rolled out midnight June 30 - July 1 2017.

claims.text: The moon landing in 1969 was staged by NASA in a Hollywood studio.
claims.rationale: Apollo 11 moon landing is confirmed by independent evidence from multiple countries including the Soviet Union.

claims.text: Mahatma Gandhi was born in Gujarat on October 2, 1869.
claims.rationale: Gandhi was born in Porbandar, Gujarat on October 2 1869.

claims.text: Mount Everest is located in China.
claims.rationale: Everest sits on the Nepal-Tibet (China) border. The summit is on the Nepal side by most definitions.

claims.text: The Reserve Bank of India was established in 1935.
claims.rationale: RBI was established on April 1 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act 1934.

claims.text: India has never won a gold medal at the Olympics.
claims.rationale: India has won gold medals in field hockey (multiple) and Abhinav Bindra won individual gold in 2008.

claims.text: WhatsApp is owned by Google.
claims.rationale: WhatsApp was acquired by Facebook (now Meta) in 2014 for approximately $19 billion.

claims.text: The Indian Constitution is the longest written constitution in the world.
claims.rationale: India's constitution with 448 articles is the longest written national constitution.

claims.text: Sugar causes hyperactivity in children.
claims.rationale: Multiple double-blind studies have found no link between sugar intake and hyperactivity in children.

claims.text: India's first Prime Minister was Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.
claims.rationale: Jawaharlal Nehru was India's first Prime Minister. Patel served as Deputy Prime Minister.

claims.text: The United States has 52 states.
claims.rationale: The United States has 50 states. A common misconception likely from counting Washington DC and Puerto Rico.

claims.text: Vitamin C cures the common cold.
claims.rationale: Cochrane reviews show Vitamin C does not prevent or cure colds in the general population.

claims.text: India is the second largest exporter of software services in the world.
claims.rationale: India is consistently the world's second largest IT services exporter after the United States.

claims.text: The Earth is approximately 6,000 years old.
claims.rationale: Scientific consensus places Earth's age at approximately 4.54 billion years based on radiometric dating.

claims.text: ISRO was founded by Vikram Sarabhai.
claims.rationale: Dr. Vikram Sarabhai is the founder of ISRO, established in 1969.

claims.text: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of New Zealand.
claims.rationale: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the northeast coast of Australia (Queensland).

claims.text: Artificial intelligence will replace most human jobs within ten years.
claims.rationale: Expert opinions vary widely. No consensus exists on the timeline or extent of AI-driven job displacement.

claims.text: India's economy will surpass that of the United States by 2075.
claims.rationale: Multiple projections (Goldman Sachs, IMF) suggest this is plausible but not certain; geopolitical factors make prediction unreliable.

claims.text: USA and Israel attacked Iran.
claims.rationale: Vague claim. US struck Iranian nuclear sites in June 2025 (Operation Midnight Hammer). Israel conducted separate strikes. Claim is too ambiguous to fully verify.

claims.text: Screen time causes depression in teenagers.
claims.rationale: Research shows correlation but causality is debated. APA cites links; other studies find the effect is small.

claims.text: Coffee is good for your health.
claims.rationale: Studies show both benefits (reduced Parkinson's, type 2 diabetes risk) and harms (sleep disruption, anxiety). Context-dependent.

claims.text: India's unemployment rate is rising.
claims.rationale: CMIE and government data disagree on methodology. Rate fluctuates and depends on definition used.

claims.text: Bitcoin will become the global reserve currency.
claims.rationale: Speculative claim. No credible evidence for or against; depends on regulatory and adoption factors.

claims.text: Remote work increases employee productivity.
claims.rationale: Mixed evidence. Stanford study found 13% productivity increase; other studies show decline especially for collaborative tasks.

claims.text: Climate change is causing more frequent cyclones in the Bay of Bengal.
claims.rationale: IMD and IPCC data show some increase in intensity but frequency trends are debated among climate scientists.

claims.text: Nuclear energy is safer than coal energy.
claims.rationale: Deaths-per-TWh statistics suggest nuclear is safer, but public perception and long-term waste storage complicate the comparison.

claims.text: Oh great, another politician promising to fix inflation — because that always works.
claims.rationale: Sarcastic statement expressing frustration. Not a verifiable factual claim.

claims.text: Yeah because banning smartphones is totally going to solve all of society's problems.
claims.rationale: Sarcastic dismissal. Cannot be fact-checked.

claims.text: Sure, I am sure eating junk food every day is perfectly healthy, said no doctor ever.
claims.rationale: Sarcastic construction with rhetorical device. Not a factual assertion.

claims.text: Because obviously giving tax cuts to billionaires solves poverty.
claims.rationale: Politically sarcastic statement. Opinion framed as sarcasm.

claims.text: Wow, what a surprise that the richest man in the room has the most to say about economic inequality.
claims.rationale: Sarcastic social commentary. Not a verifiable factual claim.

claims.text: Do you think artificial intelligence is dangerous?
claims.rationale: A question, not a factual assertion. Cannot be fact-checked.

claims.text: I personally believe that India has the best food in the world.
claims.rationale: Personal opinion. Food quality is subjective and not fact-checkable.

claims.text: Good morning everyone, hope you have a great day!
claims.rationale: Conversational greeting. Contains no factual assertion.

claims.text: The government should reduce taxes to boost economic growth.
claims.rationale: Normative policy opinion. Not a verifiable factual claim though it contains an empirical assumption.

claims.text: In my opinion, Sachin Tendulkar is the greatest cricketer of all time.
claims.rationale: Subjective judgment. 'Greatest' is not an objectively verifiable criterion.
